changes_summary: Reframed the plan around the actual root cause, settled cold-start hydration explicitly, expanded the in-scope migration to the direct `useGetTask` gallery/variant readers, defined a full-record project-scoped store contract, preserved the status-only realtime context, and explicitly deferred `useActiveTaskClips` to avoid unjustified scope growth.

success_criteria.criterion: A new `src/shared/state/realtimeStore.ts` exists with project-scoped task storage, full-record upsert/reset actions, and selector hooks for single-task and aggregate task views.

Cold-start hydration is explicitly implemented so task counts and task details are correct before the first new realtime event, using centralized query/repository paths rather than ad hoc consumer effects.
`RealtimeProvider` writes incoming task realtime events into the store at the boundary while preserving the existing Supabase connection lifecycle and status context.
`useGetTask` preserves its current disabled/sentinel contract while becoming store-backed enough that gallery and variant task readers no longer rely on broad task-query churn for visible updates.

`useTaskStatusCounts`, `usePendingGenerationTasks`, and `useGenerationTaskDetails` read from narrow store selectors or the adapted single-task path instead of depending on broad polling/invalidation for visible task-state changes.
No task-state consumer imports `RealtimeContext` or a broadcast-style realtime task API after migration; `useRealtime` may remain only as an intentional status-only API.
Store and hook tests cover task upsert, project-scoped reset, selector narrowing for unrelated task updates, aggregate status-count derivation, and empty-id disabled behavior.
Aggregate selectors return stable references for semantically unchanged object/array results, using `shallow` or equivalent equality control.
The migration keeps existing public hook signatures stable enough that downstream callers in gallery items, variant details, Tasks Pane, lightbox, and task details require little or no API churn.
`useActiveTaskClips` is explicitly classified as deferred or migrated with rationale so the milestone scope is intentional rather than accidental.
Gallery items no longer rerender on unrelated task events in a 50+ item runtime smoke test.
A per-task status surface, if present after migration, rerenders only for the matching task when observed with runtime telemetry.

assumptions: `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` remains available as a local pattern reference, but the realtime task store will be purpose-built around project-scoped task snapshots rather than generic entity persistence.
`RealtimeProvider` will continue to own the Supabase connection lifecycle, and retaining `useRealtime` as a connection-status API does not conflict with moving task data off broad query churn.
Centralized baseline hydration from task queries is acceptable as long as it is implemented inside repository/query helpers or adapted hooks, not as scattered consumer `useEffect` bridges.
`useActiveTaskClips` is outside the intended gallery/task-detail milestone unless its adoption falls out of the shared selector work with negligible additional risk.
